items.title: FromSoftware reveals final stats of new Elden Ring classes, and one of them already got a tiny nerf
items.content:
The Heavy Knight's been tweaked since we last saw them.

When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

(Image credit: FromSoftware) Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter In a new message on X, "The Everything App," FromSoftware has revealed the final names and stats of the two additional classes coming in Elden Ring's $5 Tarnished Edition DLC. The Idus Knight looks sick as ever, while the Heavy Knight appears to have gotten the gentlest, most tender of nerfs since we saw their stats leak from a Tarnished Edition playtest last year.

(Image credit: FromSoftware) (Image credit: FromSoftware) Image 1 of 2 View Original

The Heavy Knight will start at level 10, as opposed to eight in the playtest, with their two extra levels shaking out as a plus one to their Faith and Mind stats. Hardly ruinous, and I think HK will still be a new top pick for a very specific niche of builds (Hulk smash), but the change stands out to me all the same.

Due to Elden Ring 's one stat point per level system and exponentially increasing cost to level up, it's beneficial for a class to start as low level as possible, avoiding any points lost to "junk" stats not used by your build. Buildcrafters got all in a lather about HK's aggressively min-maxed stats, and now those stats have been ever so slightly less min-maxed.

It's an almost imperceptible change for your average player, but may factor into speed or challenge runs and the like. Otherwise? Heavy Knight = big guy + big sword (of the "curved great" variety) + big armor, and both of those bits of gear are new to the Tarnished Edition.

The Knight of Idus has always been more exciting to me: An agile swordsman class to give the base game Samurai some competition. They start with a scandalously swagged out new armor set, a shield of indeterminate extraction, and the previously Shadow of the Erdtree-exclusive Milady light greatsword, an extremely cool weapon that used to take hours of gameplay to acquire.

Update: Bandai Namco confirms Elden Ring's new classes and gear update is paid DLC Surely FromSoftware has something brewing besides that Switch 2 exclusive, right? I'm obsessed with a guy who keeps soloing Elden Ring Nightreign's hard mode over and over like they're trapped in a purgatory of their own making Stat-wise, Idus strikes me as a slight upgrade to the Samurai (to the same degree that the Heavy Knight was slightly nerfed): More Faith and Strength, less Arcane, Vigor, and Int, but also a lower starting level of seven.

They also introduce the nation of Idus, which has never before been mentioned in Elden Ring, and will likely be the exclusive domain of the item descriptions for the knight's armor pieces⁠. Never change, FromSoftware. The DLC will be made available on PC the same day as the Tarnished Edition's Switch 2 release: August 28.

items.title: Elden Ring Tarnished Edition reveals the Heavy Knight and Idus Knight starting classes
items.summary: Two new paths for the Tarnished
items.content:
Bandai Namco and FromSoftware have shared a new tease for the upcoming Elden Ring Tarnished Edition on Nintendo Switch 2, spotlighting two additional starting classes: the Heavy Knight and the Idus Knight. The reveal was accompanied by a poetic in-world message: "Two unseen paths, drawn by the faintest trace of grace. Tread carefully; demigods do not suffer fragile ambitions." Check out the official tweet for yourself if you haven't already.

The Heavy Knight is described as a knight clad in bulky steel armor, capable of pushing through foes with an appropriately oversized scimitar. This class starts at Level 10 with a stat spread that leans hard into physical resilience: 14 Vigor, 17 Endurance, and 15 Strength, backed by 11 Dexterity and lower magical stats.

The Idus Knight , by contrast, hails from the land of Idus and is renowned for lightweight attire and weaponry wielded with sharp dexterity. Starting at Level 7, the class balances agility and versatility with 15 Dexterity, 13 Strength, and 12 Mind, along with a more even distribution across Vigor, Endurance, and Faith.

For more news on Elden Ring, stay tuned to GamingTrend!

items.title: D&D: The Blade Of Radiance Rogue Adds A Little ‘Elden Ring’ Flavor
items.summary: Guided by the golden grace of a radiant light, the Blade of Rafiance Rogue brings Elden Ring flavors to Steinhardt's Guide.
items.content:
Guided by the golden grace of a radiant light, the Blade of Rafiance Rogue brings Elden Ring flavors to Steinhardt’s Guide.

Rogues have the shortest end of the stick when it comes to subclasses in D&D 5.5E. This is because Rogues have the longest wait between getting new features from their subclass. They get a feature at level 3, and then not again until level 9.

It really stretches out the time, and means that if your level 3 feature is even a little lackluster, or if, say, your subclass doesn’t really let you do the thing it says it does until you have a couple of features, then Rogues are in for a bit of a slog.

That’s one of the big reasons that subclasses like the Arcane Trickster are so popular, because even if you have to wait until level 9, you’re still getting new spells with your levels. But the Blade of Radiance Rogue out of Steinhardt’s Guide to the Eldritch Hunt player pack has a different take. It just makes the higher-level features feel, for the most part, truly high-level. Is it enough to counterbalance the slightly more defensive 3rd-level feature? I’ll let you judge for yourself.

One of the things the Blade of Radiance Rogue does really well is outline the fantasy of having cool sword techniques that are straight up magical. They let you gain an edge on the opponent, and they feel like something straight out of Elden Ring (instead of Bloodborne , this time). It fits the lore of Steinhardt’s Guide as well, since these Rogues are all about fighting with faith.

In game mechanics terms, this means you’ll want a good Wisdom in addition to your Dexterity. You can see it in the core level 3 subclass feature Divine Blessings . This is a big umbrella ability that gives you a pool of Divine Points which you can spend on various abilities. You get 1 + your Wisdom modifier; they refresh on Short or Long Rest, or whenever you kill a certain type of monster. Your Wisdom modifier also determines your save DC for your abilities.

What abilities, you might be wondering? At this stage, there are three. First, Armor of the Faithful, which is sort of like a single-enemy version of Sanctuary. You can spend a Divine Point, and the enemy has to save or attack someone else/waste the attack, and can’t attack you until your next turn.

Divine Inspiration , your second ability, is a little more like a ribbon. You can reroll a History, Religion, or Insight Check by spending a Divine Point, but at least you get a bonus on the reroll. The third ability, Rend the Blasphemous, is the attacking one; spend a Divine Point to make a Bonus Action Attack after you take the Attack action. It plays well with certain fighting styles, though I do think this subclass is going to be a little Bonus Action hungry, just by virtue of being a Rogue.

To round out level 3, you also gain Sanctified Champion . This, at first, appears to be a ribbon feature: you get to do a ritual on a melee weapon you’re proficient with that does piercing or slashing, and it becomes your Sanctified Blade (which you have to use for your abilities).

But it has a hidden benefit. Any weapon you designate as your Sanctified Blade gains the Finesse property for you. Even if it’s Two-Handed or Heavy. And as part of this feature, you gain proficiency with Martial weapons and Medium armor, so you can be a Rogue with a Greatsword or a Greataxe if you wanted. Which is pretty strong.

After six levels with a Bonus Action attack and a Reaction point-defense-sanctuary spell, you finally gain more abilities. These feel very much like some of the Weapon Arts you’d find in the mid-game of Elden Ring. Starting at level 9 with Righteous Armament . This feature unlocks three more uses for your Divine Points.

You get Chains of Judgment , which lets you spend a Divine Point to deal bonus damage and restrain a creature if they fail a save. Divine Retaliation lets you spend a Divine Point to attack someone who just hit you. And Erupting Blades is the real Elden Ring one, with the ability to trade in your Sneak Attack damage to one creature, to instead deal a 45-foot-long line of radiant blades that strike the ground and deal your Sneak Attack damage to any who fail a Dex save.

At level 13, Saintly Revelations gives you the ability to cast two Cleric Cantrips, as well as the Heroism, Protection from Evil and Good, and Shield spells, which you can cast on yourself at will, no spell slot or components required. If you make it to level 17, these don’t even take Concentration. This is what I’m talking about; powerful features, definitely worth the wait. I like a higher powered game myself, so your mileage may vary, but to me, this is good. That’s what level 13 SHOULD feel like.

It all caps off at level 17 with Final Judgment . This gives you two more abilities. First, you can cast Spirit Guardians once per Long Rest for free, and then for 3 Divine Points afterward. And you can imbue your Sanctified Blade with radiance, causing it to shed light and deal 2d4 extra damage. Honestly, a bit of a slowdown after the jump to level 13.

items.title: The 10 Greatest Video Game Masterpieces of the Last 50 Years, Ranked
items.summary: The video game medium has mainly taken shape in the last 50 years, thanks to triumphant masterpieces like Elden Ring, Super Mario World, and Skyrim.
items.content:
shadow-of-the-colossus-concept-art.jpg Image via Sony Computer Entertainment By Lucas Kloberdanz-Dyck Published Jul 21, 2026, 7:03 AM EDT Lucas Kloberdanz-Dyck is a writer for Collider. He grew up creating lists, stories, and worlds, which led to his love of anime and video games. He attended Sheridan College where he earned an Honours Bachelor of Game Design. Lucas and his group won 1st place for technical innovation at LevelUp Toronto 2023, and he was also an intern for the Oakville Film Festival of Arts. Sign in to your Collider account Add Us Like Like follow Follow followed Followed Thread 17 Log in Here is a fact-based summary of the story contents: Try something different: Show me the facts Explain it like I’m 5 Give me a lighthearted recap The history of video games spans back nearly a hundred years, but most people only consider it to be around 50 to 60 years old, when classic titles like Pong and Tetris shocked the world. Since then, there have been countless modern masterpieces that have further pushed the bounds of innovation in the medium, such as Red Dead Redemption 2 and God of War .

However, the true classics are still the most influential and important games in history , which begs the question: what are the greatest modern classics? This list will rank ten of the greatest video game masterpieces of the past 50 years, meaning games that came out after 1986, based on aspects such as influence, importance, legacy, design, gameplay, narrative, visuals, popularity, critical acclaim, and overall quality.

The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim Image via Bethesda The Elder Scrolls franchise is home to some of the best fantasy video games ever, and while there are more influential titles, The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim is the greatest Bethesda has to offer. Waking up in a carriage and soon to be executed, players get a second chance at life and a new purpose: kill the World Eater, Alduin, before it fulfills the prophecy and destroys the planet.

Open-world fantasy games are all the rage nowadays, and that is because of Skyrim , which paved the way for the genre with its unrelenting agency, side quests, and vast area to explore . The added mechanics and RPG elements made it an even more immersive game, which, paired with its lore and worldbuilding, makes Skyrim a phenomenal game over the past half century.

Ken and Ryu facing off in Street Fighter 2 Image via Capcom Fighting games and arcades go perfectly together, and the one game that defines both of these is Street Fighter II . When the ruthless M. Bison decides to host a worldwide fighting tournament, some of the strongest fighters on the planet come out of the woodwork and convene at its location in order to give each other a good beatdown and win the massive event.

Street Fighter II is an essential fighting and arcade game, and it is no stretch to say it is the greatest and most influential of all time . The franchise has further perfected the formula, but this game defined most of the popular mechanics in the genre , including the combo system. Street Fighter II is an important video game that deserves to be a masterpiece simply for its legacy.

Disco Elysium Game Image Via ZA/UM This list only features one indie game, but the genre is becoming increasingly popular since they are more affordable and creative. Disco Elysium is a storytelling masterpiece that puts players in the shoes of an amnesiac, drunk detective trying to solve a new murder case that has some heavy political implications behind it.

Most RPGs follow the typical system of looting and battling, but in Disco Elysium , discussion is combat. This innovative and unique gameplay has players using the main character's multiple personalities to look at the case through a different perspective, with each one having a singularly useful approach. With sharp writing and witty dialogue, Disco Elysium is also a narrative masterclass .

A warrior fighting a massive titan in Shadow of the Colossus Image via Sony Computer Entertainment Fumito Ueda is a legendary game designer, and his magnum opus, Shadow of the Colossus , also has one of the most tragic endings in the medium. After their lover dies, the protagonist makes a deal with a supernatural entity where he must kill 16 Colossi beasts in order to bring her back to life. The player must travel around the world and defeat these towering creatures in the name of love.

Shadow of the Colossus perfectly subverts the hero's journey by making every "victory" a tragic moment, highlighting the depressing tone that ultimately leads to one of the bleakest endings in video game history . The game earns its masterpiece status because of its melancholic tone, perfect ending, and innovative gameplay that cannot be replicated. Shadow of the Colossus is a unique effort that is among the best of the past 50 years.

silent-hill-2-image1 Image via Konami The first game was incredible, but Silent Hill 2 improved on everything, earning itself masterpiece status and a spot on this list. Following James Sunderland, who travels to the titular town because of a letter his deceased wife sent him, players experience his desperate search for answers, which takes him through the decaying town as he avoids the monstrous inhabitants, including Pyramid Head.

Silent Hill 2 is arguably the best horror game of all time , achieving success through its brilliant narrative, which has the enemies be representations of Sunderland's mental illnesses. The environment and monsters are a manifestation of the protagonist's subconscious and all his problems, proving the narrative is just as compelling as the gameplay. Silent Hill 2 is a masterpiece known for exploring psychological terror and human trauma through punishing gameplay, emotional worldbuilding, and a heavy atmosphere.

Grand Theft Auto's Carl 'CJ' Johnson dapping up with his friend Big Smoke in San Andreas Image via Rockstar Games The Grand Theft Auto franchise continues to push the needle with each subsequent release, but Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas is the most ambitious, with three cities to explore. After CJ returns to his city after his mother passes away, he is framed for her murder. In order to clear his name, he must travel across the state, save his family, and take back the streets.

With GTA VI soon approaching , the gaming industry will change forever, but can it compare to the best GTA game in the franchise, San Andreas ? This game is an ambitious masterpiece that was a marvel for its age , proving it was ahead of its time with its massive scale. Besides pushing the bounds of creativity, San Andreas is a well-crafted game that remains the peak of the series , for now at least.

items.title: The Convergence mod has reignited my love of Elden Ring
items.summary: Sometimes a mod can feel like playing a game for the first time.
items.content:
Feature Opinion The Convergence mod has reignited my love of Elden Ring Sometimes a mod can feel like playing a game for the first time.

It’s no secret that I adore Elden Ring. I praised it highly when it released, stating that it is Miyazaki’s magnum opus . How the game reveals new areas to you, the secrets and stories it tells, the build variety, all of it is impeccable. I spent hundreds of hours playing it. I thought I had my fill. That’s not even remotely the case. Like the long lost call of grace summoning the Tarnished back to the Lands Between, The Convergence mod has drawn me back into Elden Ring.

The Convergence mod is a mind-boggling creation by several dozen modders. It offers more than 25 starting classes, armor sets with bespoke stat increases and passives, changes talismans and Physick tears into craftable drops, adds new enemies, bosses, and weapons, and even overhauls the world with new locations while also updating dungeons.

After completing everything in Nightreign , my mate and I were eyeing off what else to do when we discovered The Convergence mod on Nexus Mods . After downloading it and the Seamless Co-op mod , we were on our way to replaying what feels like a completely different game.

While all of the core systems are still in place, the mod rejuvenates Elden Ring. Everything about the game has been touched in some way, and this is most evident with a glance at the map. Morne Castle has been upgraded with a new fortified village out the front, and while we’re still stomping through Liurnia of the Lakes, a peek at the Realm of Shadow shows an entirely new region to explore. The modded map also offers more feedback on what you have and have not completed.

But beyond the obvious changes, the mod also adds more dungeons with dastardly jumping puzzles, new encampments in once-empty areas, and brand-new bosses where once you’d find nothing but an Erdtree Avatar. After knowing a game so well, it’s exciting to wander about with no idea what’s coming.

One of the critiques you could make about Elden Ring is that armor sets don’t necessarily have a purpose beyond different stats. You would mix and match to hit the defences or weight limits you need, but that was it. The Convergence mod addresses this. Each piece of armor adds additional points to your attributes while some offer buffs, provided you avoid using specific gear. One bit of armor gave me a buff whereby using a consumable caused me to heal 15 HP every second for 60 seconds, provided I was not using a Faith or Intelligence weapon. It rewarded me for leaning into my character’s archetype.

I’ve only just scratched the surface of The Convergence mod, but it’s already dominating my thoughts. I’m even opting to play it over new games, titles I’ve been actively looking forward to trying out. I’ve been summoned back to Elden Ring thanks to The Convergence, and I couldn’t be happier.

Shacknews staff does not use generative artificial intelligence (AI) in their content. Shacknews strictly prohibits the use of its content for AI training or to generate text, including text in the style or format used for this publication. Shacknews reserves all rights to this work.

Hailing from the land down under, Sam Chandler brings a bit of the southern hemisphere flair to his work. After bouncing round a few universities, securing a bachelor degree, and entering the video game industry, he's found his new family here at Shacknews as Head of Guides. There's nothing he loves more than crafting a guide that will help someone. If you need help with a guide, or notice something not quite right, you can message him on X: @SamuelChandler

items.title: 7 Soulslike Games That Rival Elden Ring
items.summary: FromSoftware's Elden Ring might be the best soulslike game ever, but these titles stand alongside it.
items.content:
FromSoftware's Elden Ring might be the best soulslike game ever, but these titles stand alongside it.

Originally published by Game Rant Add Us On follow Follow followed Followed Like Like Thread Log in Here is a fact-based summary of the story contents: Try something different: Show me the facts Explain it like I’m 5 Give me a lighthearted recap Elden Ring is the biggest and most popular soulslike game of all time, and many would argue it stands unrivaled as the genre's crowning achievement . While few projects match the scope, depth, and addictiveness of FromSoftware's masterpiece, exceptions not only exist, but some are even better than Elden Ring in certain ways. They might offer superior combat, bosses, atmosphere, or world structure.

The Lands Between is a special place that continues to serve as home for countless players, and that won't change anytime soon. However, those same folks might want to take a trip to another soulslike realm that could very well become their favorite place away from home, and not all of these games are from FromSoftware.

Bloodborne is now over a decade old, but aside from FPS lock, the game hasn't aged a day. From the moment players step into the world, it is very clear they are in for something special, as everything from the towering structures to the eerie skybox creates a sense of darkness and intrigue that never goes away until the end credits finally roll.

While previous games had their own parry mechanics, here guns become the player's tool for deflection, setting up some incredibly visceral sequences in which they quickly dispatch and destroy anything and anyone that dares to take a swing at them. The standout, though, will always be the world and the stories within it, both taking heavy influence from Lovecraftian fiction to deliver a horrific yet enticing experience that will have players begging on their knees for more.

Team Ninja certainly took a liking to the soulslike format, essentially spending the last decade exploring the genre. While the FromSoftware inspiration is plain for all to see, Team Ninja quickly established its own niche that combines soulslike exploration with more aggressive-than-usual combat akin to the developer's earlier Ninja Gaiden games. Nioh 3 comes arguably the closest to being an Elden Ring proxy due to its open-world design, and it is fantastic; however, Nioh 2 remains the franchise's high point.

If I had to pick one way to describe Nioh 2 , it would be "a lot." Like Elden Ring , the game gives you all the tools and systems you could possibly need to take on the many challenges that await over the 50-hour campaign, and that goes beyond the variety of melee and ranged weapon types that greatly influence your playstyle.

Players can switch between three stances at any given time, all of which are viable and useful. They need to master the Burst Counter, a counter that utilizes the player's Yokai power to interrupt enemies. Then there are dozens of Soul Cores, which summon Yokai for a single attack. So much to learn, and it's so much fun.

It is pretty fair to say that when Sekiro exploded onto the scene, the entire soulslike genre was shaken down to its very core. Gone were the days of slow, methodical exploration, and in their place was fast, unforgiving combat that relied solely on reaction speed and muscle memory in a way no other game in the space had even come close to.

Mechanically, it remains at the top of the genre, demonstrating a level of precision and satisfaction that many have tried to replicate, but very few have succeeded. Then there are the boss fights, each posing a different kind of challenge to the player, and all requiring a huge amount of knowledge and patience in order to take them down. Elden Ring might have the scale, but Sekiro checks every other box, with the most important being the gameplay and the actual mechanics that players will come to learn and master over time.

Hollow Knight doesn't look like the traditional third-person soulslike many people associate the genre with, yet the gameplay and the design of the game quickly reveal that it more than fits the criteria. The way the game unfolds feels so organic and natural, something that Elden Ring definitely excels at, but here, the environments are far more claustrophobic and intimate, making the player feel one with the world throughout their long and perilous journey.

The goodness doesn't stop there, as players will find themselves tested to their absolute mechanical limits, with their platforming mechanics and their combat prowess being pushed far further than they may have expected. By the time players have scoured the map for caterpillars and conquered just about every foe in sight, they may think the adventure is coming to an end, but that's exactly when the game throws a curveball and introduces a brand-new cavern of secrets for them to explore and fall in love with all over again.

This article originally appeared on GameRant and is republished here with permission.

items.title: How to get new ELDEN RING classes, weapons, and Torrent skin customizations
items.summary: Starting on August 28, 2026, you can find the ELDEN RING Tarnished Pack on your preferred platform’s digital store or on our official Bandai Namco website.
items.content:
At long last, the magnificent universe of ELDEN RING is coming to the Nintendo Switch 2 !

Whether stepping into the Lands Between for the first time or returning for another visit to a beloved setting, this version allows you to experience this rich dark fantasy at home or on the go.

There are also several new goodies included in this Nintendo Switch 2 Tarnished Edition of ELDEN RING that people who play on PlayStation , XBOX , or PC might also want to add to their game.

Yes, all additional content included in the Nintendo Switch 2 edition of ELDEN RING can be purchased separately on PlayStation 5 and PlayStation 4 ; XBOX Series X|S and XBOX One ; and Digital PC under the name ‘ Tarnished Pack .’

The Tarnished Pack includes the following game content:

The Tarnished Pack will become available on PlayStation 5 and PlayStation 4 ; XBOX Series X|S and XBOX One ; and Digital PC on August 28, 2026 , the same day that the Tarnished Edition launches on Nintendo Switch 2 .

Starting on August 28, 2026 , you can find the ELDEN RING Tarnished Pack on your preferred platform’s digital store or on our official Bandai Namco website .

The standard price for the Tarnished Pack is € 4.99 . Prices may vary depending on local currency exchange rates.

The Tarnished Pack is a great way to add a fresh new experience to ELDEN RING .

If you’re looking for an excuse to play again or you’re embarking on the quest to become the Elden Lord for the very first time, check it out on your preferred digital store on August 28, 2026 .

ELDEN RING Tarnished Edition will be available on Nintendo Switch 2

For more information about ELDEN RING Tarnished Edition check out the latest news

ELDEN RING Tarnished Edition include the original game “ELDEN RING” , the expansion “SHADOW OF THE ERDTREE” , a new armor and customization features for Torrent’s appearance. The ELDEN RING Tarnished Edition arrives on Nintendo Switch 2 on 2026 .

items.title: What it's like beating Elden Ring twice a week for 4 years: "I think it's the greatest game that's ever been made"
items.content:
Playing FromSoftware's best RPG for a living takes you to some weird places

When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works .

(Image credit: Bandai Namco) Copy link Facebook X Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter FromSoftware is in a curious position. It released Elden Ring, its most successful and arguably best game, and ever since has exclusively released games that are very different to Elden Ring. And yes, that does include Elden Ring Nightreign, an experimental spinoff that cleverly reuses some assets and shrinks the world, then builds a PvE battle royale experience around it.

We've had Armored Core 6 and we've got The Duskbloods coming, but FromSoftware hasn't announced a new single-player dark fantasy RPG of the kind it's known for. This has left many fans revisiting the RPGs FromSoft has already released, most especially Elden Ring and its gargantuan world and DLC. It's hard to think of anybody who has gotten more out of Elden Ring than Bushy, a challenge runner who's beaten this famously long and difficult RPG over 400 times by his best estimate.

On Twitch and YouTube , Bushy is known for completing challenge runs in Elden Ring, and occasionally other games, that put restrictions on how he plays. Across hundreds of clears, he's amassed a huge collection of absurd rule sets. You have the classics, like one-hitting bosses, never dying, never getting hit, always getting hit, or kicking enemies to death. Mods, some of them commissioned by Bushy just for this purpose, also allow for stranger runs with teleportation, mismatched areas and enemies, or randomized items. Then we get to the truly specific stuff, like his most recent video: " Elden Ring But I Can Only Use White Items ".

It was this color-specific video series that pushed me to reach out to Bushy. Man , I thought, this guy has done everything . And it's easy to think that Bushy must be running out of ideas or growing sick of the game by now, but he tells me he's every bit as in love with Elden Ring as he has been for the past "3,500 to 4,000" hours of game time.

"I think it's the greatest game that's ever been made," Bushy says. "I always just have a ton of fun with it. If I didn't have that much fun with it, I wouldn't still be trying to come up with new, inventive ways to beat it. Don't get me wrong, part of that is absolutely that it's my job, and there are external forces that are encouraging me to play Elden Ring. But I think it's honestly more of a blessing than it is a curse."

7 years ago, the first Elden Ring trailer changed my life A new Elden Ring world record was set just 2 weeks ago, and I keep rewatching the same part One Bloodborne player has spent 3,500 hours helping people with one of FromSoftware's hardest bosses The release of Elden Ring Nightreign did give Bushy some space to play another game, and the Shadow of the Erdtree DLC was a refreshing injection of new content and items, but he always comes back to the game.

He considers the pessimistic read of his position. "You could look at that in a negative light," he says. "You're playing Elden Ring because you have to, because it's the only thing that makes you money. But that's not really how I look at it. Elden Ring is a game that is big enough, massive enough, deep enough, that has such a breadth of content, that having motivation and a reason to actually play it that much is really quite the blessing. I get to play it a whole lot more than other people do."

Weekly digests, tales from the communities you love, and more

Bushy's putting every last grain of Elden Ring under the magnifying glass. Like a speedrunner, he talks about individual bosses as things to "practice". His videos often see him deliberately rotate through spells, weapons, and items in order to explain and engage with multiple parts of the game rather than simply using the first winning option he can find, and his rule sets regularly lead to interesting discoveries about overlooked items and strategies. I'm reminded of MMO players running self-sufficient accounts, whose self-imposed challenges force them to play the game in novel ways.

His favorite challenge runs are those that dramatically change the game and are "higher effort" overall, like the one where he was teleported to a random checkpoint every five minutes. He also played the game with an aggression mod that made every enemy follow and attack him at all times, with basically no way to lose or avoid aggro. It didn't go well.

Now, Bushy can just throw out obscene sentences like this: "I've on average beaten the game probably twice a week since the game came out." This schedule wasn't immovable, but his channel history bears it out. I've learned not to underestimate FromSoftware fans, but I certainly haven't met anyone else who's rolled credits this many times. Additionally, Bushy says, "the longest I've gone not playing Elden Ring since it came out has probably been like three weeks."

Bushy is certainly looking forward to future FromSoftware games, but somehow reckons he's got more than enough Elden Ring challenge run ideas left in the tank. When he started playing the game for a living, building on years of streaming other games, he was "hopeful" after his early challenges got some attention. "I made lots of very bad videos and got more views than I had ever gotten at the time," he recalls. Elden Ring's massive audience was enough to turn the game into a career for him, not unlike how creators like VaatiVidya make a living by interpreting FromSoftware lore.

items.title: Summer Comes Early to the Lands Between With ELDEN RING Tarnished Edition Digital Preorders
items.summary: The official home for ELDEN RING, Armored Core, TEKKEN, DRAGON BALL, GUNDAM, PAC-MAN, and all your favorite video games!

items.title: ELDEN RING - RAGING WOLF 1/6 SCALE STATUE
items.summary: Who is the Raging Wolf? Among the first Tarnished to join the Roundtable Hold was Vargram, a warrior of great skill and ferocity. He acquired the title of the Raging Wolf from the white wolf pelt that flowed from his helm. Vargram sought to
items.content:
Who is the Raging Wolf?

Among the first Tarnished to join the Roundtable Hold was Vargram, a warrior of great skill and ferocity. He acquired the title of the Raging Wolf from the white wolf pelt that flowed from his helm. Vargram sought to become the shadow of one of the Empyreans and built his strength relentlessly as he traveled through fallow Limgrave to the golden city of Leyndell. And yet in spite of all his strength, his armor is now worn by another… An unnamed Tarnished now walks the Lands Between in the skin of the wolf. Does this warrior seek the same fate as Vargram, forever stalking at the side of an Empyrean? Will they uphold the Golden Order or supplant it for some new code? Whatever the destination, woe befall upon any who dare block the path.

The most impressive Elden Ring statue yet

Standing at 33 cm/13 inches, it’s one of the largest character statues ever produced for ELDEN RING. Crafted with the attentive supervision of FromSoftware, this full 1/6 scale figure captures each minute detail with masterful skill. The size allows perfect fidelity to the beautiful and dark fantasy atmosphere of the game, including smears of bloody red across blade of the Bastard Sword and the crest of the Iron Roundshield. From the intricate filigrees of its helm to its whisps of silvered fur blown back by an errant breeze to the humble, dusty cloth wraps protecting the feet of its greaves against the mossy stones of the path to Stoneveil Castle, it’s a portrait of the implacable will of the Tarnished. All these expertly rendered touches from sculpturer Shinya Yamaoka make this statue a perfect match to the most iconic set from one of the most iconic games of this generation.

Where can I get a Raging Wolf Set statue?

Pre-orders for the Raging Wolf 1/6 scale statue can be placed on the official Bandai Namco website. At this time, based on the production estimates of Gecco studio, these stunning PVC/ABS figures will ship to your home in Winter of 2026. If you are a true aficionado of the game, there is no better purchase to bring the world of Elden Ring to your home or office. It’s a timeless piece for your own collection or an impressive gift for a friend or loved one. And one small note, if you have not yet found this set in the Lands Between for your Tarnished, we recommend you seek the aid of Recusant Bernahl at the Volcano Manor.

Place your order for the Raging Wolf statue on the official Bandai Namco Store.

*This product is available on the Bandai Namco Store in Europe, Australia, the Middle East, India, and the United States.

items.title: ELDEN RING: SHADOW OF THE ERDTREE – Official Vinyl Soundtrack
items.summary: Follow in the foosteps of Miquella once more with this exclusive 2 disc vinyl collection, celebrating the sweeping, emotional journey of Shadow of the Erdtree. Presented with the reverence merited by a legendary expansion, this premium set
items.content:
Follow in the foosteps of Miquella once more with this exclusive 2 disc vinyl collection, celebrating the sweeping, emotional journey of Shadow of the Erdtree.

Presented with the reverence merited by a legendary expansion, this premium set is crafted for collectors, audiophiles, and Tarnished who seek to experience the Realm of Shadow through its music.

The Collection

Housed in a full color gatefold sleeve and marked with the symbol of Messmer the Impaler, each vinyl disc is pressed on heavyweight material to ensure optimal durability and warmth of playback. Whether displayed proudly on a shelf or played to relive your own quest, this set is designed as both a collector’s piece and a lasting tribute to one of the most celebrated expansions in modern gaming.

The Artists

Across 18 tracks, the soundtrack transforms the ELDEN RING experience into a powerful standalone odyssey—one carried by orchestral grandeur, ethereal choir work, and the signature melancholy that defines the worlds created by FromSoftware.

A Collector’s Treasure

This official vinyl set is an essential centerpiece for any discerning fan’s collection. Perfect for longtime followers of FromSoftware’s musical legacy or newcomers drawn into the Land of Shadow for the first time. *This vinyl is available exclusively on the Bandai Namco Store in Europe, Australia, Middle East, India and the United States

items.title: Discover the ELDEN RING Blaidd The Half-Wolf Lamp
items.summary: Summon the legendary warrior into your world with the Blaidd The Half-Wolf Lamp, a handcrafted, LED-powered tribute to one of ELDEN RING ’s most iconic character. Designed for collectors, lore enthusiasts, and Tarnished alike, this unique n
items.content:
Summon the legendary warrior into your world with the Blaidd The Half-Wolf Lamp, a handcrafted, LED-powered tribute to one of ELDEN RING’s most iconic character.

Designed for collectors, lore enthusiasts, and Tarnished alike, this unique night lamp captures the tragic loyalty and silent strength of Blaidd, Shadow of Ranni.

The lamp features an LED-illuminated base that reveals glowing rune symbols beneath Blaidd’s feet, evoking the grace-given power that guides Tarnished across the Lands Between. Rising above it, an engraved acrylic summoning panel glows softly when lit, capturing the look and feel of an in-game summon sign and the spirit-calling magic it represents. Powered through a simple USB connection, it offers effortless illumination for any desk, gaming setup, or collector’s display.

"If ever thou shouldst call upon me… I shall come."

Blaidd’s oath echoes still. Now, he stands ready, not for battle, but as a silent guardian of your space.

Bring the moonlit magic of ELDEN RING into your world, one glowing rune at a time.

*This edition is available exclusively on the Bandai Namco Store in Europe, Australia and the United States.

items.title: Why I Nearly Quit ELDEN RING
Elden Ring: Nightreign – review
items.summary: Like many gamers, I've been playing FromSoftware stuff for a long time now, dating all the way back to Demon's Souls on PS3 over 15 years ago. When Elden Ring came on the scene back in 2022, it was a very solid culmination of years of refinement of Miyazaki's "souls" formula brought to a new(ish)
items.content:
Facebook Threads Discord TikTok Instagram X Home Comics Blog Gaming Shop Patreon Discord SCORE 8.5/10 Elden Ring: Nightreign – review January 9, 2026 written by

Like many gamers, I’ve been playing FromSoftware stuff for a long time now, dating all the way back to Demon’s Souls on PS3 over 15 years ago. When Elden Ring came on the scene back in 2022, it was a very solid culmination of years of refinement of Miyazaki’s “souls” formula brought to a new(ish) IP to great success.

When Nightreign was first announced, I was skeptical about the entire idea of a Fortnite-inspired Elden Ring spin-off. I mean, I actually thought it was just a DLC until it actually came out. However, after nearly 100 hours on the game, I can faithfully say that Nightreign has become a new favorite of mine for its addictive gameplay loop and fast-paced action.

Fast-paced is the keyword here. From the moment you load into the map, you have what feels like mere moments to accomplish everything you need to accomplish. The basic idea of the game is Elden Ring gameplay and vibes, but with a Fortnite-inspired toxic ring that slowly shuts down the map every few minutes and funnels you toward the boss fight. Starting at level 1 every time, you have this limited amount of time to gain as many levels as possible (maxing at 15), collect as many weapons and buffs as you can, and face the boss with up to 2 of your best friends (or random players–or solo).

Writing this out, it doesn’t sound fun, but it ends up being a frenetic and extremely engaging time where you’re absolutely locked in for every moment. This is ultimately what makes the game so addicting, and you can expect each match to last about 45 minutes from start to finish (if you make it to the end “Nightlord” boss).

Replacing the iconic character creator from Elden Ring is a selection of 8 set characters with unique classes and abilities (plus 2 more if you get the recently released DLC). Each character fills a specific role:

Wylder is a kind of standard warrior type with a small shield and big sword, Recluse is a wizard with a big hat, Guardian (my favorite) is a defensive role with a big shield and a halberd, etc.

Initially I was disappointed at the lack of a customizable character creator, but the game is designed to be fast, simple and broad enough to accommodate the game loop. I do think it would be nice if each archetype class had an ability to customize the character, but at the very least, you can unlock a number of different skins for each one.

My biggest complaint about the game is the relic system, which is basically a gacha gambling roll for a stone that you can equip that gives you some stat-bonuses. The issue with it is that, with 10 characters in the game, you often get stones that are completely irrelevant to you, and there’s very seriously no way to influence what stones you get. Each run through the game rewards you with a handful of them, and they are 100% random. It would be nice to have some kind of crafting system where you can at least combine certain effects to make new stones that you can actually use. As it is right now, the system is more frustrating than anything else.

But that’s a minor gripe to an otherwise strangely addicting Elden Ring romp. It might seem like “SlopSouls,” but Nightreign has weirdly been one of my favorite games of the last year.

The creator of Terminal Lance. Infantry Marine turned Combat Artist turned animator turned bestselling author turned dad.

items.summary: To address the security vulnerability announced by Unity Technologies on October 3, 2025, an update to some pre-order bonuses, as well as the artwork & soundtrack application was distributed. The main game is not impacted by the security vu
items.content:
To address the security vulnerability announced by Unity Technologies on October 3, 2025, an update to some pre-order bonuses, as well as the artwork & soundtrack application was distributed.

The main game is not impacted by the security vulnerability and the update.

Thank you for your understanding.

items.content:
Bug Fixes

  • Fixed a bug wherewhere the wrong affinity or status ailments could be applied to some weapons when performing specific actions.

    [/*]

  • Fixed a bug where players could bypass being blocked by another player on any platform through unauthorized methods.

    [/*]

Regulation Ver. 1.16.1

Online play requires the player to apply this update.

Further updates will be distributed in the future for you to continue to enjoy ELDEN RING more comfortably.

Please stay tuned for more news by following our social channels or joining us on the official ELDEN RING Discord server.
